Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File-Serve 2nd Amended Complaint In Order To Name Two New Plaintiffs And The Factual Basis For Their Claims; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert T. Bryson, of Engstrom Lipscomb & Lack, Tel. 310.552.3800, for plaintiffs Gaffney, appearing via CourtCall;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SHAUN GAFFNEY, ROBIN GAFFNEY, ROBERT UNSWORTH, ROBINDIRA UNSWORTH's Motion For Leave To File/Serve 2nd Amended Complaint In Order To Name Two New Plaintiffs And The Factual Basis For Their Claims is granted. Now that the trial has been continued, there is no legal prejudice to defendants if the motion is granted and California's liberal policy allowing amendments to pleadings requires that the motion be granted. Defendants' misjoinder and unreasonable delay arguments lack merit. Nothing in this ruling precludes the trial judge from severing or bifurcating the claims of the Thobanis from some or all of the other plaintiffs. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File-Served 2nd Amended Complaint In Order To Name Two New Plaintiffs And The Factual Basis For Their Claims
Off Calendar Jan-27-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Robert T. Bryson] of the law offices of Engstrom Lipscomb & Lack (310.552.3800) appears via telephone as counsel for plaintiff/defendant/moving/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFFS SHAUN GAFFNEY, ROBIN GAFFNEY, ROBERT UNSWORTH and ROBINDIRA UNSWORTH'S Motion For Leave To File-Served Second Amended Complaint In Order To Name Two New Plaintiffs And The Factual Basis For Their Claims is DENIED without prejudice to renewing the motion if the current trial date is continued. While plaintiffs contend that the trial date will be continued, they have not filed a motion to do so and defendants appear to oppose any continuance. Absent a trial continuance, adding two new plaintiffs with distinct factual allegations from the four current plaintiffs with less than three months to trial would significantly impair defendants' ability to investigate the claims of the two new plaintiffs and engage in meaningful discovery about those claims, as well as eliminate defendants' ability to file a summary motion as to those claims. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Massachusetts Mutual Life Insurance Companys Further Response, And Production Of Documents, To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. One
Off Calendar Dec-09-2016 Off Calendar

Part 3 Of Plaintiffs January 12 2017 Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Jan-12-2017 Text Ruling

Amended Notice Correcting The Time And Dept Re Plaintiffs January 12 2017 Motion To Compel
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Steven C. Shuman of the law offices of Engstrom Lipscomb & Lack PC (310.552.3800) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiffs/moving parties,

Attorney, Austin B. Kenney of the law offices of Severson & Werson (415.677.5516) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Massachusetts Mutual Life Insurance Company

Attorney, Julie E. Ravis of the law offices of Stevens & Lee (415.677.5516) appears 'Pro Hac Vice' as co-counsel for defendant/opposing party, Massachusetts Mutual Life Insurance Company

Attorney, Tad A. Devlin of the law offices of Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck LLP (415.926.7600) appears by telephone as counsel for defendants, Elaina Setorre and Christopher Schaeffer

Both sides that are parties to the motion stipulate to Judge Pro Tem hearing the matter. The cause is submitted by both sides without argument. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Massachusetts Mutual Life Insurance Company's Further Response, And Production Of Documents, To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. One (1) is GRANTED. The Court finds the general areas of inquiry reflected by the Requests to be discoverable. Defendants objections that the requests do not seek discoverable information are overruled. Defendants also have not provided any admissible evidence in opposition with regard to the actual expense or actual burden of determining what documents would be responsive to specific requests and copying and producing such documents. To the extent that a request is overly burdensome as a result of its overbreadth, Plaintiffs have to some extent stipulated to narrow the requests by time period and geographic focus, as noted above. Nevertheless, based upon the Requests themselves, it is apparent there are different degrees of burden relating to each. The Court therefore rules and makes findings as to each, as set forth in the tentative ruling. The prevailing party shall submit via electronic mail a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim with a copy to opposing counsel.

Judge Pro Tem: David Helbraun; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302/jpt)
